chapter_title.en: Chapter 38 – The employed and the guest

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّهُ

text: فِي رَجُلٍ اسْتَأْجَرَ أَجِيراً فَأَقْعَدَهُ عَلَى مَتَاعِهِ فَسَرَقَهُ قَالَ هُوَ مُؤْتَمَنٌ

وَ قَالَ فِي رَجُلٍ أَتَى رَجُلًا فَقَالَ أَرْسَلَنِي فُلَانٌ إِلَيْكَ لِتُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِ بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا فَأَعْطَاهُ وَ صَدَّقَهُ فَلَقِيَ صَاحِبَهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ إِنَّ رَسُولَكَ أَتَانِي فَبَعَثْتُ إِلَيْكَ مَعَهُ بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا فَقَالَ مَا أَرْسَلْتُهُ إِلَيْكَ وَ مَا أَتَانِي بِشَيْ‏ءٍ وَ زَعَمَ الرَّسُولُ أَنَّهُ قَدْ أَرْسَلَهُ وَ قَدْ دَفَعَهُ إِلَيْهِ

فَقَالَ إِنْ وَجَدَ عَلَيْهِ بَيِّنَةً أَنَّهُ لَمْ يُرْسِلْهُ قُطِعَتْ يَدُهُ وَ مَعْنَى ذَلِكَ أَنْ يَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ قَدْ أَقَرَّ مَرَّةً أَنَّهُ لَمْ يُرْسِلْهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ بَيِّنَةً فَيَمِينُهُ بِاللَّهِ مَا أَرْسَلَهُ وَ يَسْتَوْفِي الْآخَرُ مِنَ الرَّسُولِ الْمَالَ
قُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ زَعَمَ أَنَّهُ إِنَّمَا حَمَلَهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ الْحَاجَةُ فَقَالَ يُقْطَعُ لِأَنَّهُ سَرَقَ مَالَ الرَّجُلِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said regarding a man employed a man for wages, so he seated him upon his belongings, but he stole it. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He was a trustee’.

And he<sup>asws</sup> said regarding a man who came to a man, so he said, ‘So and so has sent me to you, to send to him such and such’ So he gave it to him, and regarded him as truthful. So (when) he met his companions, he said to him, ‘Your messenger came to me, so I sent such and such with him, to you’. So he said, ‘I did not sent him to you, and he has not come to me with anything. And the messenger claim that he has sent him, and he had handed over (the items) to him.

So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If proof is found against him that he did not send him, his (messenger’s) hand would be cut, and the meaning of that is he happenned to be the messenger who has accepted once that he (the sender) did not send him; and if proof is not found, so he (the sender) would have to swear by Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that he did not send him (the messenger), and the other one can retrieve the goods from the messenger’.

I said, ‘What is your<sup>asws</sup> view if he (the messenger) claims that he had carried it away due to the need’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘His hand would be cut because he stole the goods of the man’.
